description: Rocking and Rolling at Head Crater

tokens: Okay, let me turn (to get the switch on the panel on the left side of the spacecraft).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll have to come towards you (in order to get enough room behind him to get his PLSS around).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Tell you what; why don't you release my
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) pulling mine and then when you go down (on hands and knees) to go out, I'll take yours up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me...Hold my knee against that...That Velcro doesn't hold worth a hoot.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's the way; you're centered good this trip.
Move a little bit to the left.
(Pause) Here, let me get that antenna as you go.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Move a little bit to your right.
And I'll be standing by to get the
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) going off; did the (jett) bag go off (the porch)?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You want to hand me the LEC?
(Correcting himself) I mean the left-hand-side stowage; you want to get rid of it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (I'll) open the hatch a little more and give it a kick.
(Pause) And I'm standing by for the LEC.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ah, I've got to bring it up (farther, because Al can't reach out far enough to get the LEC from Pete's outstretched hand).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Down to campus (for their final geology exam).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: One of these contrast charts fell down here yesterday, Al.
So there's only two good ones; the other one's too dirty.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It (the dust clinging to the chart) doesn't rap off; it just...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, ready to transfer ETB when you are.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Coming right after it, right now.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks like the lock on the LEC is kind of jammed with...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You have to let it out easier.
All right, just lower it some more.
All right, hold it right there.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, two things I'm going to learn to dislike: and one of them's the TV cable and the other one's the S-band antenna cable.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, before egress, would you confirm that the TV circuit breaker is out?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: In all the (garbled) activity yesterday, I forgot all about him sitting here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Getting this (16 mm sequence) camera set.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'll tell you, these Teflon bags don't hold up too well in a vacuum.
"In a lunar environment;" I'll put it that way.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Yeah, they tend to have fatigue failures along the cracks (that is, along the side folds) when you go to open them up.
I notice the two bags that I put in the SRC yesterday were that way.
And, let me get back here (into the sunlight) just a second.
(Reading) "Pass LEC; ETB transfer; geology Prep; stow on HTC: contrast charts, extension handle, hammer, small shovel, and gnomon."
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Reading) "Place SRC 2 on MESA; attach weigh bag to scale; attach saddlebag to LMP." There's one thing that's bothering me; I don't have that saddlebag.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Offering to watch Al come out through the hatch) Let me check.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Wait a minute while I try to close this hatch.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Right now, this stuff...(correcting himself) this "material" around the spacecraft reminds me...In this Sun angle, looking into the Sun, (it's) a very rich brown color, (and it) kind of reminds me of a good plowed field.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looking down-Sun, it's still the same old ash gray; very light, white, ash gray.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: How about let's get a Surveyor parts bag here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm going to pull down those little side visors.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think Al and I both find that these little side Sun visors are extremely handy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) this strap down (on the Surveyor parts bag).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Almost cold today (in the LM shadow) on mid-cooling.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm going to set the tether (safety line) right there and...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay; and I'll put it (the Surveyor parts bag) on you here, babe.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Do) you want me to stand low or high?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, got that one on - (garbled) the side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You know, other than the large-size rocks, (it's) very, very difficult to determine a contact around here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We're putting the parts bag on Pete right now, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That Comm is super; it sounds like you're right inside my helmet.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's the best sim(ulated EVA) we've had.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I wonder what happened since yesterday?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think everybody learns a little bit...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That bag's on there; now, let's see what else (is on the checklist).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I need to get the (bolt-cutting) tool.
(Pause) And before you put the tool in (the Surveyor parts bag), we got to cut a TV cable.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Cut the TV cable below the adapter, about 1 inch and then...(correcting himself) that's 1 foot below the adapter, and then stow the TV camera in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Long Pause) Hey, look at that Surveyor, Al.
That's not anywheres near as bad a slope (as it had seemed during EVA-1).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Houston, that Surveyor looks a lot better today.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, now that the Sun's up on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, wait a minute; leave it (the TV camera) right on there (on the tripod) for a second.
Come on over and put the thing (the bolt cutter) in my back (that is, in the parts bag) and let's mount our (Hasselblad) cameras and then the ETB is empty (to receive the TV camera).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Or either that, (or) bring it (the TV camera) back...I'm sorry.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think you got the right...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Look at that part number on the side of the tool.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, Houston, while he's putting the tool on, it's a very interesting thing.
There is a angular rock that's literally 6 inches from the engine exhaust skirt.
It's just sitting on the lunar surface; and I really find it hard to believe that the engine exhaust couldn't blow that rock away.

It's only about 3-1/2 inches by 3-1/2 inches; and it's not stuck in the ground; it's just sitting there loosely about 6 inches from the engine bell; and, of course, the ground is blasted clean all the way around it and yet the engine exhaust blast didn't blow that rock away.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Were you able to get a photo of that in the first EVA?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: No, we'll get that right now.
Is there one (that is, a saddlebag) in here, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Pete and Al, for your reference on the photos, your shadow length now is 18 foot.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Pete) Here's a couple of sneaky bags (that is, saddlebags) for us.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, also if you would, before you start that traverse, would you get a good photo of the solar wind to show us how that foil is wrapped around?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Okay, Houston, that won't take a second.
Okay, let me attach this bag to...Would you attach this bag to me, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to slide that off at the same time.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Okay, I'll tell you what, if you go put one camera...Here, the saddlebag's on...if you'd put one (70mm Hasselblad) camera on...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...Go get the TV, I'll mount this other gear on here (that is, on the HTC).
My compliments to the man that packed that SRC box; it looks just like the training boxes.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hand me those tongs a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait; hold on just a second.
Hold on; let's see what this is.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: ...one of those parts bags (he may mean the remnants of a weigh bag).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) (Talking to the core tube) Come on; get out of there.
If I didn't know better, I'd say there was solar wind up here (pause) that blows hard enough to blow sample bags in the wrong direction.
(Pause) Okay, Al, three core tubes in the deal (that is, in the HTC).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Chart fits right in its spot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now here's the other thing here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Why don't I hook those on there, too.
That thing (probably the pack of flat sample bags)'s a pain in the neck over there, the way they spread out in the vacuum.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And I got the gas sample tube here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now I need to get that safety line.
Okay; I'll put that over here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, I'll get the safety line.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll put my (70mm) camera on; we'll put the TV camera in the ETB; and away we go.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you get your (70mm) camera, I'll put that TV camera in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, from the local terrain, Houston, as you know it right now, and with the polarizing filter, have you got any particular place enroute to the ALSEP or to Head Crater that you'd like any polarizing pictures taken?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, from the local terrain, Houston, as you know it right now, and with the polarizing filter, have you got any particular place enroute to the ALSEP or to Head Crater that you'd like any polarizing pictures taken?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We'll get back to you on that.
Press on now with the nominal plan right now.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (To Al) Here, let me have it (the TV camera).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, let me fold the handle.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I got it as far as I can.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, we have no preference on that.
(That is, Houston has no preference with regard to a location for the pictures to be taken with the polarizing filter.) Go ahead and take it as called out for in the cuff checklist.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Stick it in the ETB and we'll screw with it (meaning the TV) later.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, Houston, one TV camera in the bag.
And our plan of attack is...Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Get) one picture of that rock under the descent stage;...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...(then) grab the Hand Tool Carrier and head for the solar wind and grab a picture of that.
In the meantime, I'll lope off to the ALSEP and check the SIDE.
I'll meet you at Point 1 at Head Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And, Al, have you gotten the readings on the contrast charts?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Not yet; and I plan to do that real quick.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, Pete's on his way to the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Making a mis-identification) Roger, Al; we copy.
And at 30 minutes into the EVA, you're pretty close to the nominal time line.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Pause) Can the guy with the seismometer hear me running?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled; possibly something about a camera setting) (Long Pause) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we're watching you down here on the seismic data.
Looks as though you're really thundering right by it.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Yeah, I ground to a halt there to
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And we were able to copy (that is, sense) your rest and now that you're moving again (on the seismometer).
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: The (CCIG) status is...Oops, I'm going to get dust in it.
The cover is off, and it's pointed up at the sky at about a 60-degree angle.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Do not touch it right now, Pete.
Which way is that pointing, relative to east-west?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: It is pointed down-Sun (that is, west).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Pete, no need to change the configuration; let's press on.
(Pause) We copy that you've stopped on the seismic.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Meet you at the Head Crater, pal.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, boy, is that...I want that rock.
That is a dandy extra grapefruit-size-type goody.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (I need to) find a crater with a shadow in it first.
(Pause) Okay, Houston, I'm approaching a crater now and I'm going to put the contrast chart in it.
One (photo taken with the chart placed) on each side (of the contact between sunlight and shadow).
One on the sunny side, one on the shadow side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There's the one on the sunny side.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Man, have I got the grapefruit rock of all grapefruit rocks!
That's got to come home in the spacecraft; it'll never fit in the rock box.
(Pause) Okay, Houston, I'll tell you what I'm going to do.
I'm going to wind up at the right place at Head Crater; and, while I'm waiting for Al, I'll roll a boulder for you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm now looking at the contrast...(Pause) (Sub vocal, taking a picture of the contrast chart on a level surface) Five (feet focus) and one (picture).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can you give us a mark when you roll?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That crater is...(Answering Houston) Yeah, I sure will.
Head Crater is, by golly, a rather steep crater; a lot steeper than it looks from out the LM.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm looking at the contrast chart in the Sun and I can see all the different shades.
Okay, now I've taken a photo of it; now I'll look at the one in the shadow.
In the shadow, I can see...Well, it kind of depends on how close I am.
If I'm within about 3 feet of it or 4 feet of it, I can see all six shades.
I'll take a picture here, then I'll back up.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me ask you a question, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I presume whatever's a convenient size for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: That's what I'm holding in my hand and these other rocks that I was talking to you about are pretty well buried, and they're pretty large.
I don't think I could get one of them going.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Grapefruit-size or any size is fine.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Yeah, I'll stand still; go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Houston, on my mark, gonna roll it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (About one second intervals) Now it's just rolling.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We'll get a reading on it for you.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, Houston, I'm looking at the contrast chart in the shadow and, as I mentioned, at 3 feet I can see all six.
If I back up maybe to 10 feet, as long as I stand here a moment and adapt my eyes, I can see all six, also.
Now, the thing that seems to have the biggest effect on it is how low the Sun is.
The Sun is high now and so I don't have to squint my eyes particularly looking in that direction (of the Sun).
Yesterday, looking into the same crater - even though it wouldn't be any darker in there - because the Sun was there, I would never be able to adapt.
Right now, I can see all six marks, and I've taken the photographs.
Going to go out and do solar wind now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can't see a lot of difference in visibility here as on Earth, really.

The only major difference I've noticed is the fact that when you're out here on this area, if you look cross-Sun, the Moon appears one color; if you look down-Sun, it's another; if you look up-Sun, it's another.
But looking into shadows or anything else like that, it's pretty much the same as (looking into shadow) on Earth.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Okay, I'll take some pictures here of the solar wind for you.
(Long Pause) It doesn't look as pronounced wrapped around the pole now that I get out here, Houston.
It looks pretty much like yesterday.
I guess it was sort of an optical illusion from inside the spacecraft.
(Pause) I took a couple of pictures of it, but I don't think there's anything unusual going on there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: And, Pete, if it's convenient and you can find another rock there and give her a heave, Experiments sure would like to see another one.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Okay, I was setting up my rock hole and all that good things for the polarizing light.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, I was looking at a rock that has small crystals in it.
One of them is shining very, very bright green, like ginger-ale-bottle green.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, are you on your way?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's affirm; I'm now making sure that everything is in the Hand Tool Carrier here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: As I was working around the Hand Tool Carrier a moment ago, the canvas bag (the big sample bag in the middle of the HTC) came loose.
It took me about 2 minutes to put it back together again; it came off the metal sides.
And it looks like those clips that hold it on are going to be completely inadequate and I expect that we're going to have some trouble with it all day today.
Maybe you ought to think about fixing it before the next time (that is, before Apollo 13).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Do you think that once you put a little weight in it, she'll hold better?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I don't know; they don't seem to have a lot of friction on the sides there, and that bag just floats around.
It needs to be more firmly attached some way.
transcript.speaker: Bean
